methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Deep Atomic is a pre-seed nuclear energy company founded by William G.J. Theron. The nuclear energy sector comprises a modest but growing peer universe of 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms, including advanced reactor developers such as TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy. This limited peer set reflects the capital intensity and regulatory constraints characterizing the sector.

A peer-median methodology is not applicable to Deep Atomic's valuation. The analysis identified 8 same-sector peers overall, but none met the minimum threshold of three comparable datapoints when filtered by stage (pre-seed), adjacent stage cohort, or similar valuation tier. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial distortion given the heterogeneity of technology maturity, capital requirements, and regulatory pathways across nuclear energy subsegments. The scarcity of pre-seed nuclear transactions limits the precision of traditional peer multiples at this stage.

Without a sufficient comparable universe meeting narrow filter criteria, conventional equity multiples triangulation cannot be reliably executed. A defensible valuation would require alternative methods: founder track record assessment, technology risk weighting, comparable early-stage financing terms from the private peer set if transaction data is available, or venture-standard dilution-adjusted return expectations. The key uncertainty is the absence of pricing precedent for pre-seed nuclear technology in the current dataset, making any valuation estimate dependent on non-comparable methodologies and sector-specific risk adjustments.
